LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANT DOMINIC TSE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING AS FOLLOWS: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT IS DENIED, FAILURE TO SHIFT THE BURDEN OF PROOF. THE RECORDS ON WHICH THE DEFENDANT'S EXPERT RELIED WERE NOT PROPERLY ADMITTED UNDER THE BUSINESS RECORDS EXCEPTION TO THE HEARSAY RULE. SEE GARIBAY VS. HEMMAT (2008) 161 CAL.APP. 4TH 735. PLAINTIFF'S EVIDENTIARY OBJECTIONS ARE SUSTAINED. DEFENDANT'S REPLY AND DECLARATIONS STRICKEN AS UNTIMELY. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT.(302/CWW)JUDGE: CHARLOTTE WALTER WOOLARD; COURT REPORTER: KENT GUBBINE, CSR #5797

Revised Notice Of Motion And Motion To Determine Good Faith Of Settlement [Ccp 877.6]
Off Calendar Dec-17-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
PLAINTIFF LIDIYA DEMICHEVA'S REVISED MOTION TO DETERMINE GOOD FAITH OF SETTLEMENT (C.C.P. 877.6(a)(1)) [WITH DEFENDANT HOWMEDICA OSTEONICS CORP.].

APPEARANCES: Steven A. Fabro, Esq. of Law Offices of Steven A. Fabro 391-6850 for pltf & moving party; Michael D. McConathy, Esq. of Lynch, Gilardi & Grummer 397-2800 for deft & opposing party Dominic Tse, M.D.

RULING: The Court adopted its tentative ruling - Granted. Order signed in open court. (302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard, Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MISCELLANEOUS ORDER TO SHOW CAUSE
Off Calendar Dec-09-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Order to Show Cause to Defendant's Counsel Ralph A. Campillo for failure to comply with San Francisco Superior Court Early Settlement Program Policies and Procedures No. 4. (Administrative fee) is ordered off calendar and dissolved/Per Early Settlement Program. Proceedings not reported. (212)

Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Determine Good Faith Settlement
Off Calendar Nov-19-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF LYDIA DEMICHEVA'S MOTION TO DETERMINE GOOD FAITH SETTLEMENT (with defendant Howmedica Osteonics Corp.). Appearances: Claudia Lozano of Lynch Gilardi & Grummer (397.2800) for defendant and responding party; Steven A Fabbro of the Law Offices of Steven A. Fabbro (391.6850) for plaintiff and moving party. The court denied plaintiff's request for a continuance. The matter was argued and the court adopted a tentative ruling as follows: denied without prejudice based on insufficient showing re: Tech-Bilt Factor (value of case). Prevailing party to submit a proposed form of order. (302/PHA) Judge: Paul H. Alvarado, Reporter: Kent Gubbine CSR #5797

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Aug-27-2010 Set For Trial On Apr-25-11 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206; Settlement Conference On Apr-08-11 At 03:30 P.M. In Dept 502

MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT
Off Calendar Jun-30-2010 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT DOMINIC TSE MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT. Appearances: Steven Fabbro, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 391-6850 appearing for Plaintiff and opposing party; Claudia Lozano, Esq. of Lynch, Gilardi & Rummer, (415) 397-2800 appearing for Defendant and Moving party. Argued and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion for Summary Judgment is denied, failure to shift the burden of proof. The records on which the defendant's expert relied were not properly admitted under the business records exception to the hearsay rule. See Garibay vs. Hemmat (2008) 161 Cal.App. 4th 735. Plaintiff's evidentiary objections are sustained. Defendant's reply and declarations stricken as untimely. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW)Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797

MINI MINUTES: PLAINTIFF LIDIYA DEMICHEVA Motion to Amend Complaint and Vacate Trial Date. Appearances: Steven A. Fabbro, Esq. Attorney at Law, (415) 391-6850 appearing for Plaintiff and moving party. The Court adopted its tentative ruling as follows: Motion to amend is granted. Plaintiff has until April 20, 2010, to file and serve amended complaint. No opposition filed. Motion to vacate the trial date to be made in Department 206. Order signed in open Court.(302/CWW) Judge: Charlotte Walter Woolard; Court Reporter: Kent Gubbine, CSR #5797
